</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) Basics</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) helps us organize code in a way that makes it easier to work with. In interviews, you’ll often be asked about OOP principles.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Let's break them down simply:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Here are the 4 Pillars of OOP you should know:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Class &amp; Object</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A class is a blueprint. An object is an instance of a class.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">class Dog:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    def <em class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="_${appText}_">_init</em>_(self, name):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        <a dir="auto" href="http://self.name" title="http://self.name" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">self.name</a> = name
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    def bark(self):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return f"{<a dir="auto" href="http://self.name" title="http://self.name" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">self.name</a>} says woof!"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">dog1 = Dog("Bruno")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(dog1.bark())
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Output:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Bruno says woof!
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. Inheritance</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Allows a class to inherit properties and methods from another class.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">class Animal:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    def speak(self):
</span><span class="">        re…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_B0249851957B58755F6601C3816850E5"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[4:39 pm, 26/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Let's move to the next important topic in the Python Learning Series
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Strings in Python</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A string is simply a sequence of characters, enclosed in either single quotes ' ' or double quotes " ".
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">name = "John"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">city = 'New York'
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">How to create multi-line strings?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Use triple quotes:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">message = '''Hello,
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Welcome to Python!'''
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Common String Operations:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Accessing Characters</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Strings are like lists of characters — you can use indexing:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">text = "Python"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(text[0])  # Output: 'P'
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(text[-1]) # Output: 'n' (last character)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. String Slicing</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You can grab parts (slices) of a string:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">text = "Python"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(text[0:2])  # Output: 'Py'
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(text[2:])   # Output: 'thon'
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(text[:4])   # Output: 'Pyth'
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="">Tip: text[start:end] grabs characters fr…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">4:39 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div </span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: D) orange</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">First, fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"].
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Then fruits.append("orange") adds "orange" at the end of the list.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Now, the list becomes ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "orange"].
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">fruits[3] means the item at index 3 (remember, indexing starts from 0).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">So, fruits[3] is "orange".

copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: B) 9</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The range(1, 6, 2) generates numbers starting from 1, up to but not including 6, with a step of 2. So, the numbers generated are: 1, 3, 5.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The loop iterates over these values and adds them to sum:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">First iteration: sum = 0 + 1 = 1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Second iteration: sum = 1 + 3 = 4
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Third iteration: sum = 4 + 5 = 9
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The final value of sum is 9, and that is printed.

_as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[7:11 pm, 27/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: C) 5</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The function add takes two parameters: a and b, where b has a default value of 2.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">When we call add(3), we only pass one argument.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">So, a = 3 and b = 2 (default value).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The function returns 3 + 2 = 5.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Thus, the output is 5.

x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[7:38 am, 28/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let's move to the next important topic in the Python Learning Series
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Input, Output, and Basic String Formatting</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Taking Input from the User:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">In Python, you can use the input() function to take input from the user.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">name = input("Enter your name: ")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print("Hello,", name)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">input() always returns data as a string.</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">Whatever the user types will be stored in name.</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Converting Input to Other Types:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">If you want the input as a number, you have to convert it.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Example:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">age = int(input("Enter your age: "))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print("You are", age, "years old")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Here, int() is used to convert the input from string to integer.</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Similarly:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">float() for decimal numbers</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">bool() for true/false values (careful with this one)</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Printing Output:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_CD718C0A78FA08E586A8E33F5D525CAA"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[8:56 am, 28/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: B) 55</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">input() always returns a string.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">So when the user enters 5, num becomes the string "5".
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">num * 2 means repeating the string "5" two times → "55".
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">It does not perform mathematical addition unless you first convert the input to an integer.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Thus, the output is 55.</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Most of you responded with 10 which is pretty much expected, If you want users to enter a number and treat it like a number, you must convert it to integer like this:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">num = int(input("Enter a number: "))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(num * 2)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Now if they enter 5,
</span><span class="x1lliihq">int(5) * 2 = 10 (integer multiplication). Hope this clarifies most of the doubts.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

data-pre-plain-text="[11:30 am, 28/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let's move to the next important topic in the Python Learning Series:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Lists, Tuples, Sets, and Dictionaries</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Lists</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A list is an ordered collection that is mutable, meaning you can change its content after creation.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">It can hold items of different data types (integers, strings, etc.).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">my_list = [1, 2, 3, "hello", 5.6]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(my_list)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Lists are indexable: my_list[0] gives the first element.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Important methods:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">append(): Adds an item at the end of the list.</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">remove(): Removes the first occurrence of an item.</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">pop(): Removes and returns an item at a specific index.</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. Tuples</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A tuple is like a list, but immutable, meaning once created, its content cannot be changed.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">They are often used for data that shouldn't change.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="">my_tu…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_8FE7E9A2DFFBFB678F297F6F99FCCE09"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[4:24 pm, 28/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: c) 3</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">my_list is a list containing [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">tuple(my_list) converts the list into a tuple: (1, 2, 3, 4, 5).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">my_tuple[2] accesses the element at index 2 (remember indexing starts from 0), so:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Index 0 → 1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Index 1 → 2
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Index 2 → 3
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Thus, the correct answer is c) 3.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[9:28 pm, 28/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let's move to the next topic in our Python Learning Series:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">String Manipulation</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Strings are one of the most commonly used data types in Python.  They represent sequences of characters and are incredibly flexible for text-based operations.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Let’s explore some key operations and methods to manipulate strings.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Basic String Operations:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You can access individual characters in a string using an index: string[index].
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">String indices start at 0.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Example: "Python"[0] gives 'P'.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. Concatenation:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You can concatenate strings using the + operator:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">str1 = "Hello"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">str2 = "World"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">result = str1 + " " + str2
</span><span class="x1lliihq"># Output: "Hello World"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">3. Repetition:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You can repeat strings using the * operator:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">"Python" * 3
</span><span class="x1lliihq">#Output: 'PythonPythonPython'
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">4. Slicing:</strong>
…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">9:28 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>9:28 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; data-pre-plain-text="[12:22 pm, 29/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: c) [2, 6, 10]</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The list comprehension loops through the list nums.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">if x % 2 != 0 filters odd numbers → [1, 3, 5].
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Each of these is multiplied by 2 → [2, 6, 10].
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">12:22 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">12:22 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_5B87829C36EF813B5F26C78F24BD31D7"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[1:41 pm, 29/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Let's now move to the next important topic in the Python Learning Series
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">File Handling in Python</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is File Handling?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">File handling allows Python to read from and write to files—text files, CSVs, logs, etc.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">It’s used in almost every real-world project: whether it's storing user data, reading configurations, or writing logs.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Basic File Operations:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Open a file</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">file = open("example.txt", "r")  # 'r' for read mode
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. Read from a file</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">content = file.read()
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(content)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">3. Write to a file</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">file = open("example.txt", "w")  # 'w' for write mode
</span><span class="x1lliihq">file.write("Hello, Python!")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">file.close()
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">4. Append to a file</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">file = open("example.txt", "a")  # 'a' for append mode
</span><span class="x1lliihq">file.write("\nNew Line")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">5. Close the file</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">file.close()
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

data-pre-plain-text="[7:40 pm, 29/4/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: C</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">ZeroDivisionError is raised when you try to divide a number by zero — it's a built-in Python exception.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">This was little tricky but some of you still got right
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0, nums) → filters out even numbers → [2, 4]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">reduce(lambda x, y: x + y, [2, 4]) → 2 + 4 = 6
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">So the correct answer is 6

data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_C800D33B4466A6E9D70245E811F7D439"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[9:10 am, 1/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: A. 5</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">In this code, the global keyword is used to refer to the global variable total_items inside the function add_item().
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">When we call add_item(5), the global total_items is updated to 0 + 5. So, when we print it, the output is 5.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">9:10 am</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>9:10 am</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_7AE02112DDEA42FE691672DD485A7777"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[9:47 am, 1/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let’s move to the next topic in the Python Learning Series
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Working with Dates &amp; Time</strong> <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="📅" draggable="false" class="b76 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="📅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -20px;"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="⏰" draggable="false" class="b1 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="⏰" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -40px -40px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Importing the datetime module</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">To work with dates and times, Python provides a built-in module called datetime.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">import datetime
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. Getting the current date and time</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">from datetime import datetime
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">now = datetime.now()
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(now)  # Shows full date and time
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(<a dir="auto" href="http://now.date" title="http://now.date" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">now.date</a>())  # Only date part
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(now.time())  # Only time part
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">3. Creating a specific date or time</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">from datetime import datetime
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">my_birthday = datetime(1995, 8, 25)  # Year, Month, Day
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(my_birthday)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You can also add hours, minutes, and seconds:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">specific_time = datetime(2023, 10, 5, 15, 30, 45)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">4. Formatting Dates (strptime &amp; strftime)</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_7119BC71BA198EBFF7C5832BBFDE6AA9"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[1:27 pm, 1/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: A. April 30, 2025</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Let's break it down:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. from datetime import datetime</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Imports the datetime class from the datetime module.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. date_str = "<a dir="auto" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt" style="cursor: pointer;">30-04-2025</a>"</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">This is a date in the format DD-MM-YYYY.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">3. datetime.strptime(date_str, "%d-%m-%Y")</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">This converts the string into a datetime object using the correct format.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">strptime() stands for "string parse time".
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">4. date_obj.strftime("%B %d, %Y")</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">This formats the date object to a readable string:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">%B → Full month name (April)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">%d → Day with leading zero (30)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">%Y → Full year (2025)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Final output: "April 30, 2025"
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">1:27 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">1:27 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_C4AB5E4E768920B5B9B819C3F1167B0B"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[1:42 pm, 1/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let's move to the next topic in the Python Learning Series.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Classes and Objects</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is a Class?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A class is a blueprint — just like an architect’s plan for building houses.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">In Python, you create a class using the class keyword.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">class Dog:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    # This is a class
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    pass
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">This doesn't do much yet — it's just an empty plan.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is an Object?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">An object is something created from the class — like an actual house built from the plan.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">my_dog = Dog()
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Now, my_dog is an object (or instance) of the Dog class.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Let’s Build a Useful Example</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Define the Class</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">class Dog:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    def <em class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="_${appText}_">_init</em>_(self, name, breed):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        <a dir="auto" href="http://self.name" title="http://self.name" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">self.name</a> = name       # Attribute
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        self.breed = breed     # Attribute
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    def bark(self):
</span><span class="">        print(f"{<a dir="auto" href="http://self.name" title="http://self.name" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">self.name</a>} says: Woof!")
…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">1:42 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>1:42 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_4A12C7B5BBACEB4CF61DBCF4846D0344"><div class="_anz0 _amkz message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd _amlv"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div aria-label="Poll from Python Programming 5:49 pm What will be the output of the following code?

class Student:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name

    def greet(self):
        return &quot;Hello, &quot; + self.name

s1 = Student(&quot;Amit&quot;)
print(s1.greet()) Top vote counts: Hello, Student: 0, Hello, Amit: 0, Amit: 0, Error: greet() takes 0 positional arguments: 0. " class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xctv8m x18d9i69 xbm2j3o"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[5:49 pm, 1/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><div class="x1iorvi4 x11lfxj5 xjkvuk6 x135b78x"><span dir="ltr" class="x1s688f x1jchvi3 _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">What will be the output of the following code?
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">class Student:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    def <em class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="_${appText}_">_init</em>_(self, name):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        <a dir="auto" href="http://self.name" title="http://self.name" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">self.name</a> = name
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    def greet(self):

</span><span class="x1lliihq">s1 = Student("Amit") creates an object s1 with name = "Amit".
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">s1.greet() calls the greet() method, which returns "Hello, " + <a dir="auto" href="http://self.name" title="http://self.name" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">self.name</a>.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">So, the output is "Hello, Amit".
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">6:56 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">6:56 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; </span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">For those of you who joined the channel recently, you should first go through the previous topics from here: <a dir="auto" href="https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VaiM08SDuMRaGKd9Wv0L/1527" title="https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VaiM08SDuMRaGKd9Wv0L/1527" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VaiM08SDuMRaGKd9Wv0L/1527</a>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Inheritance and Polymorphism</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is Inheritance?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Inheritance is an important concept in Object-Oriented Programming (OOP). It allows one class to inherit the properties and methods of another class. This helps us create a hierarchy of classes, where a base class (or parent class) is extended by one or more derived classes (child classes).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">In simple terms, inheritance lets you reuse code by creating new classes based on existing ones.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Real-World Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="">Imagine you have a parent class called Animal and two child classes:…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">7:29 am</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>7:29 am</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_67517101D96FB5FF74B2E809FF0F807C"><div class="_anz0 _amkz message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd _amlv"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div aria-label="Poll from Python Programming 10:33 am Which of the following best demonstrates polymorphism in Python? data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_5366D01D1FC93B07FB21819064965EC7"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[7:34 am, 3/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: A</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A decorator is used to modify or extend the behavior of a function without changing its actual code. It wraps the original function and adds extra behavior before or after the call.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">7:34 am</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">7:34 am</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_FD18920DBD0DE2BC3D86C4D20C84082F"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[11:30 am, 3/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let's move to the next important topic in the Python Learning Series:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Generators &amp; Iterators</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. What are Iterators?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">An iterator is an object that allows you to iterate over a sequence of values. It implements two special methods:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><em class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="_${appText}_">_iter</em>_() → Returns the iterator object itself
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><em class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="_${appText}_">_next</em>_() → Returns the next value; raises StopIteration when exhausted
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">my_list = [1, 2, 3]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">iterator = iter(my_list)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(next(iterator))  # 1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(next(iterator))  # 2
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(next(iterator))  # 3
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. What are Generators?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A generator is a simpler way to create iterators using functions and the yield keyword.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Key Differences:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">Uses yield instead of return</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">Maintains state between calls</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">More memory-efficient for large data</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def countdown(n):
</span><span class="">    wh…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">11:30 am</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">11:30 am</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_2EB957EBE9A521DF32D1844871968FA9"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[5:23 pm, 3/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: C</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Mixing multiple responsibilities in a single function violates the Single Responsibility Principle and makes code harder to read and maintain. Each function should have one clear task.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">5:23 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">5:23 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; --lineHeight: 8.4609px; --8dd7yt: 0.0615em; --hxtmnb: -0.0615em;">4/5/2025</span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6"><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_A53D3CE300B24FF8332D054490B3D32E"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[12:05 pm, 4/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: B) element.get_text()</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">get_text() is the correct method to retrieve the inner text of an element.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">text() and innerText() are not valid in BeautifulSoup.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">extract() removes the element from the parse tree.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">12:05 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">12:05 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_9DCB490E43415628A16C931DF446B623"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[3:55 pm, 4/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let's move to the next important topic in the Python Learning Series
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Working with APIs</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is an API?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">An API (Application Programming Interface) allows communication between two software systems. In Python, we commonly interact with APIs over HTTP to request or send data—think of APIs like waiters who deliver your order (data) from a restaurant (server).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">The requests Library</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Python’s requests library makes it simple to send HTTP requests like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Basic Example - GET Request</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">import requests
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">response = requests.get("<a dir="auto" href="https://api.github.com" title="https://api.github.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">https://api.github.com</a>")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(response.status_code)
</span><span class="x1lliihq"># 200 means success
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(response.json())
</span><span class="x1lliihq"># Print response content in JSON
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">get(): Fetch data from the API.</span></li>
</ul></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><ul class="x13faqbe xeaf4i8 x1n2onr6 x1gslohp x12nagc x13fj5qh" dir="ltr"><li dir="auto" class="x1g0dm76 x1jieuv1 xo7wnuk x1gznph8 x1rkpvhg xlu7um4 xm78dhd x1r4uxqn"><span class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="- ">.json(): Converts the res</span></li></ul>…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">3:55 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>3:55 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[6:22 pm, 5/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Correct Answer: B) <a dir="auto" href="http://requests.post" title="http://requests.post" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">requests.post</a>()</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><a dir="auto" href="http://requests.post" title="http://requests.post" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text x1bvjpef x1lku1pv x11iimpl xbvygy2 x1wp9yj1 x1e2wovf x1ph7ams x17f7hit x1k74hu9 xn69kzl xrys4gj xhmieyt">requests.post</a>() is used to send data (like form data or JSON) to an API.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">requests.get() is only used to retrieve data.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">fetch() and send() are not valid functions in the requests library.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you got it right</strong></span></span><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">6:22 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">6:22 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_20E4BA92BBE41D6579D2E34D3B69AA95"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[10:06 pm, 5/5/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Now, let's move to the next topic in the Python Learning Series
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Data Analysis Using Pandas</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. What is Pandas?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Pandas is a Python library used for working with structured data. It makes it easy to analyze, clean, explore, and manipulate data.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. Key Concepts:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">a. DataFrame and Series</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Series</strong>: A one-dimensional labeled array (like a column).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Example:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">pd.Series([1, 2, 3])
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">DataFrame</strong>: A two-dimensional labeled data structure with rows and columns (like an Excel sheet).
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Example:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">pd.DataFrame({'Name': ['Tom', 'Jerry'], 'Age': [28, 22]})
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">3. Reading Data</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">From CSV:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">From Excel:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">df = pd.read_excel('data.xlsx')
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">4. Basic Operations</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">View first/last few rows</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">df.head()     # First 5 rows
</span><span class="x1lliihq">df.tail()     # Last 5 rows
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Check </strong>…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">10:06 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>10:06 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px;
