_amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[11:21 pm, 3/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Here are the Answers for the previous quizzes</strong> <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q1. What will the following code output?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">d = {"a": 1, "b": 2}
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(d.get("c"))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. None</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">.get("c") safely tries to access the key "c". Since it doesn't exist, it returns None instead of throwing an error.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. Which one of these is NOT allowed as a key in a dictionary?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: D. List</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Dictionary keys must be immutable (unchangeable) types like strings, numbers, or tuples. Lists are mutable, so they can’t be used as keys.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. What is the output of the following code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">s = {1, 2, 3, 2, 4}
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(len(s))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. 4</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Sets only store unique values. The duplicate 2 is ignored, so the set becomes {1, 2, 3, 4} — which has 4 items.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q4. Which method returns all key-value pairs in a dictionary?</strong>
</span><span class="">
…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">11:21 pm</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>11:21 pm</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; --lineHeight: 8.4609px; --8dd7yt: 0.0615em; --hxtmnb: -0.0615em;">5/6/2025</span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6"><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_8C3E88F4D2A506109F93ABEBBFAE282E"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[8:34 am, 5/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 6: Conditionals (if, elif, else)</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">In Python, conditional statements allow your code to make decisions.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="💡" draggable="false" class="b74 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="💡" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -40px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What Are Conditionals?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">They help your program execute certain code blocks only when specific conditions are true.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Syntax</strong> :
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">if condition:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    # Code runs if condition is True
</span><span class="x1lliihq">elif another_condition:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    # Runs if previous conditions were False, this one is True
</span><span class="x1lliihq">else:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    # Runs if none of the above conditions are True
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🧠" draggable="false" class="b137 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🧠" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example</strong> :
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">age = 18
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">if age &gt;= 18:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("You’re an adult.")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">elif age &gt; 13:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("You’re a teenager.")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">else:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("You’re a child.")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Output:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You’re an adult.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🎯" draggable="false" class="b27 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🎯" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -40px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Mini Project: Guess the Number Game</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="">Let’s build a small game using what …</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s xk50ysn x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">8:34 am</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto"><span class="x2fvf9 xwklpps">Edited</span>8:34 am</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; </span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q1. What will this code output?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">x = 5
</span><span class="x1lliihq">if x &gt; 10:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("A")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">else:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("B")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Since 5 is not greater than 10, the else block runs and prints "B".
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. Which operator is used to check equality in conditionals?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. ==</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">== checks if two values are equal. = is for assignment.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. What will this code print?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">x = 7
</span><span class="x1lliihq">if x &lt; 10:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("Less than 10")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">elif x &lt; 20:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("Less than 20")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: A. Less than 10</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The if condition is True, so it executes and skips the elif.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q4. Which of the following is a correct if statement?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> Answer: C. if x == 5:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">This is the correct syntax to check if x is equal to 5.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q5. What keyword is used when all other conditions fail?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

--lineHeight: 8.4609px; --8dd7yt: 0.0615em; --hxtmnb: -0.0615em;">7/6/2025</span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6"><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_64B8E925C77CE528CB82F5D37EA929DB"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[11:43 pm, 7/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 7: Loops in Python (for and while)</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Loops are one of the most powerful tools in programming. They help us repeat tasks without writing repetitive code.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔁" draggable="false" class="b78 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔁" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -40px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">for Loop</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Use a for loop when you know how many times you want to repeat something.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><code class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="```${appText}```">for i in range(5):
    print(i)</code>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🟢" draggable="false" class="b100 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🟢" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Output</strong> :
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">0
</span><span class="x1lliihq">1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">2
</span><span class="x1lliihq">3
</span><span class="x1lliihq">4
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> range(5) creates a sequence from 0 to 4
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> i takes each value one by one
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔁" draggable="false" class="b78 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔁" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -40px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">while Loop</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Use a while loop when you want to repeat until a condition is no longer true.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><code class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="```${appText}```">count = 1
while count &lt;= 5:
    print(count)
    count += 1</code>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🟢" draggable="false" class="b100 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🟢" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Output</strong> :
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">2
</span><span class="x1lliihq">3
</span><span class="x1lliihq">4
</span><span class="x1lliihq">5
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔨" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔨" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -60px 0px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Mini Projects to Practice</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Multiplication Table Generator</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><code class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="```${appText}```">num = int(input("Enter a number: "))

xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[12:14 am, 11/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Here are the Answers for the previous quizzes</strong> <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q1. What is the output of the following code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">x = 0
</span><span class="x1lliihq">while x &lt; 3:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print(x)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    x += 1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: A. 0 1 2</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The loop runs while x is less than 3. So it prints 0, 1, and 2 before exiting.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. What does range(1, 6) produce?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">range(1, 6) includes 1 and excludes 6. So it returns 1 to 5.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. Which of the following is used to exit a loop early?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. break</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The break statement stops a loop immediately, even if the loop condition is still true.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q4. What is the output of this code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">i = 0
</span><span class="x1lliihq">while i &lt; 3:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    i += 1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print(i)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. 1 2 3</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Since i is incremented before printing, the values printed are 1, 2, and 3.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">React with <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="❤️" draggable="false" class="b8 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="❤️" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px 0px;"> if you're ready for the next topic</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="">P…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">12:14 am</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">12:14 am</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; --lineHeight: 8.4609px; --8dd7yt: 0.0615em; --hxtmnb: -0.0615em;">12/6/2025</span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6"><div tabindex="-1" class="" role="row"><div tabindex="-1" class="_amjv _aotl" data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_4FB557315D9AFC55478627042DF7B493"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[8:53 am, 12/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 8: Functions in Python</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🤔" draggable="false" class="b101 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🤔" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is a Function?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A function is a reusable block of code that performs a specific task.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Think of it as a machine: you give it some input (arguments), it processes something, and then gives you output.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Defining a Function</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Here’s the basic structure:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def greet():
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("Hello, Python learner!")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">To call or run the function:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">greet()
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🟢" draggable="false" class="b100 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🟢" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Output:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Hello, Python learner!
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Function with Parameters</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You can make your function accept values using parameters:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def greet(name):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print(f"Hello, {name}!")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">greet("Sam")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🟢" draggable="false" class="b100 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🟢" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Output:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Hello, Sam!
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Return Values</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Functions can return values instead of just printing them:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def add(a, b):
</span><span class="">    re…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">8:53 am</span></span></span></div></div><div class="x1n2onr6 x1n327nk x18mqm2i xhsvlbd x14z9mp xz62fqu xdwrcjd"><div class="x13yyeie xx3o462 xuxw1ft x78zum5 x6s0dn4 x12lo8hy x152skdk"><span class="x1rg5ohu x16dsc37" dir="auto">8:53 am</span></div></div></div></div></div></div><span class=""></span><div class="_amlr"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 x1q0g3np x6s0dn4 _amj_"></div></div><div class="x78zum5 xbfrwjf x8k05lb xeq5yr9 x1n2onr6 xrr41r3 xqcrz7y"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="x141l45o x1h3r9g6 x1hx0egp x78zum5 x1q0g3np xl56j7k x18nleir" tabindex="-1"><div class="x1n2onr6 x9f619 x1rg5ohu x1okw0bk x193iq5w xjwt4uw x1izp4mc xqf7i6v xe6rjme xhslqc4 x2b8uid x1k43qru x1dymr41 x12ptayk x17h9p7f xjker3h x148chh3"><span dir="auto" class="x184q3qc xqxll94 x1s928wv xhkezso x1gmr53x x1cpjm7i x1fgarty x1943h6x x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs x1lliihq x1fj9vlw xhslqc4 x1pg5gke xjb2p0i xfjzk2p xl2ypbo x1ic7a3i" style="--fontSize: 12px; </span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q1. What keyword is used to define a function in Python?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. def</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The def keyword is used to define a function in Python. Example: def greet():
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. What is the output of this code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def add(a, b):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    return a + b
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(add(3, 4))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: A. 7</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The function returns 3 + 4, which is 7, and print() displays it.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. What happens if a function has no return statement?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. It returns None</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">All functions in Python return None by default if there’s no return statement.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q4. Which of the following is a correct way to call this function?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def greet(name):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("Hello", name)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. greet("Alice")</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The function expects one parameter, and "Alice" is passed correctly.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[8:37 am, 16/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 9: Function Arguments in Python</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🤔" draggable="false" class="b101 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🤔" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Why are Arguments Important?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Arguments allow us to pass data into a function so it can act on different values — making the function dynamic instead of static.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Types of Function Arguments</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">1. Positional Arguments</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">These are the most common — the order matters.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><code class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="```${appText}```">def greet(name, age):
    print(f"{name} is {age} years old.")

greet("Alex", 25) </code>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">2. Keyword Arguments</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You specify the name of the argument, so order doesn’t matter.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">greet(age=25, name="Alex")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">3. Default Arguments</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Provide a default value so the argument becomes optional.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><code class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="```${appText}```">def greet(name, city="Delhi"):
    print(f"{name} is from {city}")

greet("Riya")             # Uses d</code>…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s </span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. Sam Welcome!</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Since msg has a default value and no value is passed for it, it uses "Welcome!". So the function prints both name and msg.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. What does *args allow you to do in a function?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. Pass a variable number of positional arguments</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">*args packs multiple positional arguments into a tuple. It allows functions to accept more arguments than originally defined.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. What is the correct way to define a function with default values?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. def func(x, y=10):</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="">In Python, parameters with default values must come after non-default ones. So x is required, y is optional.…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_00EF485140548C062920B7EF2E3990F5"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[1:38 am, 17/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 10: Recursion Basics</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🤔" draggable="false" class="b101 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🤔" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is Recursion?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Recursion is when a function calls itself to solve smaller parts of a problem — ideal for tasks that can be broken down into repetitive subproblems.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Basic Recursive Structure</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def function_name():
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    # base condition
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    if condition:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return something
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    else:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return function_name()  # recursive call
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Without a base condition, the function will keep calling itself forever — causing a RecursionError.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="💡" draggable="false" class="b74 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="💡" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -40px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example 1: Factorial Using Recursion</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def factorial(n):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    if n == 0 or n == 1:  # base case
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return 1
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    else:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return n * factorial(n - 1)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(factorial(5))  # Output: 120
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Here’s how it works:</strong>
</span><span class="">
…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span def mystery(n):
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    else:
        return n + mystery(n - 1)

print(mystery(3)) Top vote counts: 6: 4740, 3: 416, 0: 348, Error: 334. </span><span class="x1lliihq">def mystery(n):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    if n == 0:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return 0
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    else:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return n + mystery(n - 1)

data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_B76DCF13AD97BD20E7FF56F7DF02583E"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[10:59 pm, 17/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Here are the Answers for the previous quizzes</strong> <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q1. What is required for a recursive function to stop calling itself?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. A base case</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A base case is the condition under which the recursive function stops calling itself. Without it, the function would recurse infinitely and crash.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. What will be the output of this code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">def mystery(n):
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    if n == 0:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return 0
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    else:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">        return n + mystery(n - 1)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(mystery(3))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. 6</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">This computes the sum 3 + 2 + 1 + 0 = 6.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">It works like this:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">mystery(3) → 3 + mystery(2) → 3 + 2 + mystery(1) → 3 + 2 + 1 + mystery(0) → 0
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. What is the main risk of writing a recursive function without a base case?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. It results in a RecursionError</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="">Without a base case, the function k…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_40F2352C1A68612FB1B44810B4FCC90F"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[5:59 pm, 18/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:<img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="⚡" draggable="false" class="b4 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="⚡" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -20px;"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🐍" draggable="false" class="b35 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🐍" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px 0px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 11: Lambda, map(), and filter()</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🧠" draggable="false" class="b137 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🧠" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">What is a Lambda Function?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A lambda function is an anonymous function written in one line using the lambda keyword.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Syntax:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">lambda arguments: expression
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔸" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔸" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example:</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">square = lambda x: x ** 2
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(square(5))  # Output: 25
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><blockquote dir="ltr" class="x1rg5ohu x1n2onr6 xh8yej3 xbs004d x1gslohp x12nagc _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="&gt; "><div class="x10l6tqk x1o0tod x13vifvy x5yr21d x1g8rjiy x1plhrm9 xkqq1k2 x91jh78 x1xkn691 x4oqio7"></div><div class="x1rg5ohu x1gx403c xj0a0fe xeaf4i8"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> Use lambdas when you need a simple function for a short time — usually as an argument to map(), filter(), or sorted().
</div></blockquote></span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🧩" draggable="false" class="b137 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🧩" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">map() — Apply a Function to All Items</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Takes a function and an iterable, and applies the function to every item in the iterable.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">nums = [1, 2, 3, 4]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">squares = list(map(lambda x: x ** 2, nums))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(squares)  # Output: [1, 4, 9, 16]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🧼" draggable="false" class="b138 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🧼" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -60px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">filter() — Filter Items Based on a Condition</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q1. What is the output of this code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">square = lambda x: x ** 2
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(square(3))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. 9</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">The lambda function returns x ** 2.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">So square(3) → 3 ** 2 → 9.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. Which of the following best describes map() in Python?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. Applies a function to all items in an iterable</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">map(func, iterable) runs func on every element in iterable and returns a new iterable.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. What will be the result of the following code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">nums = [1, 2, 3, 4]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">result = list(map(lambda x: x + 10, nums))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(result)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. [11, 12, 13, 14]</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Each element is increased by 10 → [1+10, 2+10, 3+10, 4+10]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q4. What does filter() return?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. Only elements that evaluate to True based on a function</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">

style="background-position: -60px 0px;"></div></div><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 xeuugli x2lwn1j xozqiw3 xamitd3 x12fk4p8" style="flex-shrink: 0;"><div class="x1c4vz4f xs83m0k xdl72j9 x1g77sc7 x78zum5 xozqiw3 x1oa3qoh x12fk4p8 xeuugli x2lwn1j x1nhvcw1 xdt5ytf x1cy8zhl xlup9mm x6ikm8r x10wlt62 xdwrcjd x1p8j9ns"><div class="x1hshjfz xlup9mm x6s0dn4 x78zum5"><span class="x1f6kntn data-id="false_120363310706406515@newsletter_26A2BAC90F5CA13F5AAA16555DC49140"><div class="_anz0 message-in focusable-list-item _amjy _amjz _amj- _as4s _amjw x1klvx2g xahtqtb"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[9:50 am, 23/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:<img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="⚡" draggable="false" class="b4 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="⚡" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -20px;"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🐍" draggable="false" class="b35 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🐍" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px 0px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 12: List Comprehensions in Python</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A list comprehension is a concise way to create lists by applying an expression to each item in an iterable.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Basic Syntax</strong> :
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">[expression for item in iterable if condition]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔸" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔸" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example 1: Square All Numbers</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">nums = [1, 2, 3, 4]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">squares = [x**2 for x in nums]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(squares)  # Output: [1, 4, 9, 16]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔸" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔸" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example 2: Filter Even Numbers</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">evens = [x for x in nums if x % 2 == 0]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(evens)  # Output: [2, 4, 6]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔸" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔸" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example 3: Convert Strings to Uppercase</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">names = ["alice", "bob", "charlie"]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">upper_names = [name.upper() for name in names]
</span><span class="x1lliihq">print(upper_names)  # Output: ['ALICE', 'BOB', 'CHARLIE']
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class=""><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔁" draggable="false" class="b78 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔁" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -40px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Equivalent Using</strong>…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class=""></span><div class="_amk4 false _amkd"><div class="_amk6 _amlo"><span aria-label="Python Programming:"></span><div><div><div><div class="x9f619 x1hx0egp x1yuel0x x1yrsyyn xizg8k xu9hqtb xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[9:24 am, 25/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq">Today, Let’s move on to the next topic in the Python Coding Challenge:<img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="⚡" draggable="false" class="b4 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="⚡" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -20px;"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🐍" draggable="false" class="b35 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🐍" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px 0px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Day 13: Error Handling in Python</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">When something unexpected happens — like dividing by zero or reading a missing file — Python raises an exception.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">If not handled, your program crashes.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">The try...except Block</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">try:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    # risky code
</span><span class="x1lliihq">except SomeError:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    # handle error
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔸" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔸" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -80px -60px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Example</strong> :
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">try:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    x = int(input("Enter a number: "))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print(10 / x)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">except ZeroDivisionError:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("You can't divide by zero!")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">except ValueError:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("Please enter a valid number.")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="🔹" draggable="false" class="b80 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="🔹" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: 0px -80px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">else and finally</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">else: runs if no exception was raised
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">finally: runs no matter what (cleanup code)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">try:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    num = int(input("Enter number: "))
</span><span class="x1lliihq">except ValueError:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("Not a number!")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">else:
</span><span class="">    pri…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read more</div><span class=""><span class="x3nfvp2 xxymvpz xlshs6z xqtp20y xexx8yu x11lfxj5 x18d9i69 x135b78x x12lo8hy x152skdk" aria-hidden="true"><span class="x1c4vz4f x2lah0s">9:24 xwib8y2"><div class="copyable-text" data-pre-plain-text="[2:06 pm, 27/6/2025] Python Programming: "><div class="_akbu"><span dir="ltr" class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" style="min-height: 0px;"><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Here are the answers for the previous quizzes</strong> <img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q1. What is the purpose of a try...except block?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: B. To handle errors gracefully and prevent crashes</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">A try...except block lets you run code that might fail and catch the error without crashing the whole program.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q2. What type of error does this code raise?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">num = int("abc")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. ValueError</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">You can’t convert "abc" to an integer, so Python throws a ValueError.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q3. What is printed by this code?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">try:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print(10 / 0)
</span><span class="x1lliihq">except ZeroDivisionError:
</span><span class="x1lliihq">    print("Math error!")
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> <strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Answer: C. Math error!</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">Division by zero triggers a ZeroDivisionError, which is caught by the except block.
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><strong class="_ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-app-text-template="*${appText}*">Q4. What is the role of the finally block?</strong>
</span><span class="x1lliihq">
</span><span class="x1lliihq"><img crossorigin="anonymous" alt="✅" draggable="false" class="b6 emoji apple  _ao3e selectable-text copyable-text" data-plain-text="✅" tabindex="-1" src="data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7" style="background-position: -20px -20px;"> Answer: C. Runs regardless of whether an exception occurs
</span><span class="">Exp…</span></span><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="xuxw1ft x1ypdohk x1rg5ohu xo1mcw5 x1xlr1w8 x1hl2dhg xt0b8zv xbvygy2 x17f7hit xp30eni xhmieyt read-more-button"> Read
